opp.name: McCormick_SRM+Intake_April'26
opp.next_step: 5/14/26 MQ Attempting to re-engage with Paul to schedule deep dive discovery and demo of iRisk. 4/21/26 MQ Scheduling deep dive discovery with Paul Charrow. 4/8/26 MQ Paul Charrow will review IRisk info with stakeholders. Shedule deep dive discovery call to prep for demonstration.
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma meetings on this opp — one account-level ProcureCon follow-up with Joyce Whitney exists but was unrecorded and tied to a different opp. No customer AI cue.
• McCormick is described as having fragmented tools, spreadsheets, and manual processes — looking for help on SRM/iRisk. No AI keywords in the SF narrative.
• Marc Quessenberry's April next steps have Paul Charrow reviewing iRisk info and scheduling a deeper discovery. SRM-driven cadence with AI not foregrounded.

contacts.title: Senior Procurement Manager - Global Design & Transformation

items.quote: 5/14/26 MQ Attempting to re-engage with Paul to schedule deep dive discovery and demo of iRisk.

items.quote: No approval chain, procurement channel, or evaluation timeline documented.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified impact has been captured. The Next_Step__c notes (8 Apr, 21 Apr, 14 May 2026) reference scheduling a discovery call to discuss iRisk, but the call has not occurred. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) carry pain or ROI data, and the one account-level call (ProcureCon follow-up, 21 Mar 2025) predates this opportunity and its scope. Without discovery, the quantified value case does not exist. The immediate move is to execute the deep-dive discovery call the owner has been attempting since mid-May and surface the operational pain and financial impact iSupplier, iRisk, and Merlin Intake would address.

sources: SF Next_Step__c (14 May 2026, no buyer response)
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Paul Charrow (Senior Procurement Manager - the deal owner's manager & Transformation) is the single contact role and the target of the discovery call being scheduled, but there is no evidence of advocacy, access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, or active engagement—he has not responded to the owner's outreach since 14 May 2026 (43 days ago). A champion would be driving the agenda, introducing Zycus to other stakeholders, and pushing back on inertia or competitors; none of that has occurred. The account roster includes senior procurement leaders (Amy Couch, David Wright, Luke Rogers) who could become champions if engaged, but none are confirmed as involved. The discovery call is the first opportunity to assess Paul's level of advocacy and to identify a multi-threaded champion network.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty, and no free-text fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c) carry competitive intelligence. No buyer calls on this opp scope have occurred to surface whether McCormick is benchmarking Zycus against SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, or an incumbent tool, or whether this is a sole-source greenfield. The Next_Step__c notes suggest McCormick is evaluating iRisk and iSupplier, implying some form of vendor selection is underway, but the competitive landscape is unmapped. The discovery call must ask what tools they use today, what alternatives they are considering, and what their evaluation timeline is.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. The Next_Step__c notes (8 Apr, 21 Apr, 14 May 2026) reference scheduling a discovery call to discuss iRisk and iSupplier, implying McCormick has an interest in supplier risk management and intake automation, but no pain owner, pain statement, or quantified impact (cost, cycle time, error rate, headcount) has been captured. The one account-level call (ProcureCon follow-up, 21 Mar 2025) predates this opportunity and its scope. Without discovery, we can only infer that McCormick's procurement team sees a potential fit for iSupplier, iRisk, and Merlin Intake, but we do not know what problem they are solving, what manual processes or incumbent tools they are replacing, or what happens if they do nothing. The discovery call must surface the specific pain and its owner.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are unknown. No MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, or procurement channel has been discussed. No Salesforce fields carry paper-process details, and no calls on this opp scope have occurred to surface these requirements. For a $120k SaaS+services deal at a large enterprise, legal redlines, InfoSec approval, and a formal SOW are standard, but the timeline and stakeholders are unmapped. The discovery call is the first opportunity to ask about their contracting process, whether an MSA is already in place (McCormick may have prior Zycus relationships), and what InfoSec/legal gates will follow a technical fit.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. The single contact role, Paul Charrow (Senior Procurement Manager - the deal owner's manager & Transformation), is an operational stakeholder, not a budget authority. No Salesforce fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) name a CFO, procurement director, or C-level sponsor. The account roster includes senior procurement leaders (Amy Couch, Procurement Director; David Wright, Global Procurement Finance Director; Luke Rogers, Director of Category Management) who could be budget authorities, but none are confirmed as involved in this deal. The discovery call being scheduled is the opportunity to map the approval chain and identify who controls the commercial decision for a $120k SRM+Intake+iRisk investment.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. No buyer calls have occurred on this opportunity's scope, and no Salesforce fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) describe the approval chain or procurement channel. The Next_Step__c note (8 Apr 2026) mentions Paul Charrow reviewing iRisk info with stakeholders, suggesting a multi-person buying committee exists, but no names, roles, or sequence have been mapped. The account is a large enterprise (McCormick & Company, Inc), so a formal procurement process, legal review, and InfoSec approval are likely, but none are confirmed. The discovery call must map who evaluates, who approves, and what gates (RFP, security review, contracting) lie ahead.

sources: No buyer calls on this opp scope
decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria have not been documented. No RFP, RFI, use-case review, or formal evaluation has occurred. The Next_Step__c notes indicate Paul Charrow will review iRisk info with stakeholders (8 Apr 2026), implying an internal evaluation is planned, but no criteria have been shared. No Salesforce fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c, Decision_Criteria__c) carry fitment requirements. Without the discovery call, we do not know what McCormick is evaluating SRM+Intake+iRisk against—incumbent tools, manual processes, or a competitive shortlist. The discovery call must surface their evaluation framework, technical requirements, and decision timeline.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 308 (close 2027-04-30). Created 3 Apr 2026, 84 days old. Time in Qualified stage: at least 84 days (no stage-date series available). Last verified activity 19 Jun 2026 (7 days ago) keeps the deal from cold status, but the owner has been attempting to schedule a discovery call since 14 May 2026 with no buyer engagement on that planned next step. No forward slip visible in field history, but the close date is a placeholder over 10 months out. The pace is not the issue; the stall on the planned discovery is.
evidence: Owner attempting re-engagement since 14 May 2026 (43 days ago) per Next_Step__c
No buyer calls on this opp scope (one account-level ProcureCon follow-up call 21 Mar 2025 with Joyce Whitney, predating this opp and unrelated to its scope)
Single contact role: Paul Charrow (Senior Procurement Manager - Global Design & Transformation), no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker mapped
No MEDDPICC coverage: no pain quantified, no decision process, no champion evidence
Products in scope: iSupplier, iRisk, Merlin Intake; no pricing or commercials discussion yet
Pipeline forecast is defensible for an early Qualified stage with a 10-month runway, but the deal needs discovery momentum to stay live
north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal stuck for 43 days awaiting discovery call with no buyer engagement. Single-threaded to procurement manager, no champion or pain quantified. Needs immediate re-engagement to validate if this opportunity is real or should be disqualified.

flags: Zero MEDDPICC coverage: no pain quantified, no Economic Buyer identified, no decision process mapped, no champion evidence, and no competitive intelligence. The deal cannot progress to the relevant stakeholder or Shortlisted without discovery depth. The discovery call being attempted is the single highest-leverage move to unlock all MEDDPICC elements.
best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded deal stalled on a discovery call for 43 days with no MEDDPICC coverage and no buyer engagement. Immediate priorities: execute discovery to surface pain and decision process, multi-thread to senior procurement leadership, and identify the Economic Buyer.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been identified from any source. The deal is either sole-source, greenfield (no incumbent tool being replaced), or the competitive landscape has not yet been discovered. Without a buyer discovery call, we cannot determine whether McCormick is benchmarking Zycus against Ariba, Coupa, GEP, or another vendor, or whether they are evaluating iSupplier/iRisk/Merlin Intake as a first-time SRM+Intake automation investment. The discovery call must surface what tools they use today and what alternatives they are considering.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is in the product scope, indicating McCormick has some interest in AI-powered intake automation. However, no AIS score, no buyer calls, and no discovery conversation have occurred to confirm AI appetite, use-case fit, or the strength of the Merlin narrative. The AI positioning is entirely untested.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: No customer expectations or evaluation criteria have been captured. The deal is pre-discovery; no business requirements, use-case fit, or technical evaluation has occurred. The Next_Step__c notes indicate Paul Charrow will review iRisk info with stakeholders (8 Apr 2026), implying McCormick is assessing fit internally, but no criteria have been shared with Zycus. Without discovery, we cannot assess alignment on SRM capabilities, intake automation, risk-scoring algorithms, Merlin AI integration, or any other technical or business requirement.
